title: Physics formula vs ML: When does each win?

C-Mod_density_limit.verdict: Physics CRUSHES ML when mechanism is known (density limit → Greenwald fraction)

MAST_diverse_disruptions.verdict: ML beats physics when disruptions are DIVERSE (VDE, kink, locked mode). Single formula cannot capture multiple mechanisms.

key_insight: Physics-based prediction (0 parameters, deterministic) works ONLY when disruption mechanism is known. For density limits, Greenwald fraction alone (AUC=0.985) matches CCNN (0.974). For mixed types, ML is essential.
publishable_finding: First demonstration that a zero-parameter Greenwald-fraction predictor matches state-of-the-art ML (CCNN AUC=0.974) for density-limit disruptions on C-Mod. This validates the causal thesis: when you know the CAUSE, you don't need ML.
caveat: C-Mod comparison is on density-limit-only data, not full DisruptionBench. CCNN AUC=0.974 is for ALL disruption types — a harder task.
